description: Add missing unit tests for browser extension modules: offscreen WebSocket utility functions, resource/prompt dispatch, confirmation badge management, and background message handlers.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for offscreen WebSocket utility functions
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the pure utility functions in the offscreen module so that URL validation and conversion logic are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover isValidWsOrigin: valid ws:// URL with matching host passes, wss:// passes, mismatched host rejects, non-ws protocol rejects, wrong path rejects, malformed URL rejects
Tests cover wsToHttpBase: converts ws://localhost:9515/ws to http://localhost:9515, converts wss:// to https://
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts. isValidWsOrigin (line 53) validates WebSocket URLs against an HTTP base URL — checks protocol (ws:/wss:), host match, and path (/ws). wsToHttpBase (line 79) is a one-liner regex replace. These are not currently exported — export them for testing. The offscreen module has complex WebSocket state machine code, but only these two pure functions need unit tests (the state machine is integration-tested by E2E). See platform/browser-extension/src/tab-matching.test.ts for the test setup pattern (vitest with chrome API mocks).

userStories.title: Add unit tests for resource-prompt-dispatch.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the resource read and prompt render dispatch functions so that error paths and adapter validation are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover handleResourceRead: missing plugin returns JSONRPC error, unfrozen adapter returns integrity error, missing resource URI in adapter returns error, successful resource read returns content, dispatchWithTabFallback is used correctly

Tests cover handlePromptRender: missing plugin returns JSONRPC error, unfrozen adapter returns integrity error, missing prompt in adapter returns error, argument coercion to strings, successful prompt render returns messages
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/browser-extension/src/resource-prompt-dispatch.ts (248 lines). The module exports handleResourceRead and handlePromptRender. These are structurally similar to tool-dispatch.ts which IS tested (see platform/browser-extension/src/tool-dispatch.test.ts for the exact mocking pattern). The key challenge is mocking chrome.scripting.executeScript which runs code in the MAIN world — the test should mock the chrome API and verify the correct function/args are passed. The adapter integrity checks (frozen check at line 40, isReady check) and error code propagation (-32002, JSONRPC_INVALID_PARAMS) are the most important paths to test. Use opus because this requires understanding the dispatch-helpers module and chrome API mocking pattern.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for confirmation-badge.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the confirmation badge management so that timer coordination and badge clearing logic are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover notifyConfirmationRequest: sets badge count, creates Chrome notification, sets timeout for auto-clear
Tests cover clearConfirmationBadge: decrements badge count, clears notification, clears individual timeout
Tests cover clearAllConfirmationBadges: resets badge to empty, clears all timeouts, clears all notifications
Tests cover double-clear prevention: clearing an already-cleared badge does not underflow count
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/browser-extension/src/confirmation-badge.ts (125 lines). This module manages Chrome extension badge text to show pending confirmation count. It uses chrome.action.setBadgeText, chrome.notifications.create/clear, and setTimeout/clearTimeout for auto-dismissal. Use vi.useFakeTimers() to control timeouts. Mock chrome.action and chrome.notifications APIs. The module maintains internal state (badge count, timeout map) — test state transitions through the public API. See platform/browser-extension/src/rate-limiter.test.ts for similar stateful module testing patterns.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for background-message-handlers.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the background message handler routing so that connection state management and message forwarding are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover handleWsState: connect sets wsConnected=true in storage, disconnect sets wsConnected=false and clears tab state cache and confirmation badges
Tests cover handlePluginLogs: validates content-script data structure, forwards valid logs to server via sendToServer
Tests cover handleToolProgress: dispatches progress to notifyDispatchProgress with correct dispatchId
Tests cover handleConfirmationResponse: forwards response to pending confirmation handler
Mock chrome.storage.session, sendToServer, and other imported dependencies
userStories.notes: The handlers are in platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (270 lines). The module exports individual handler functions for each message type. handleWsState manages connection/disconnection transitions — on disconnect it calls clearTabStateCache and clearAllConfirmationBadges. handlePluginLogs does runtime validation of the incoming data shape before forwarding. Mock the chrome.storage.session API and imported modules (messaging.ts sendToServer, tab-state.ts clearTabStateCache, confirmation-badge.ts clearAllConfirmationBadges). See platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.test.ts for the mocking pattern.
